ksa_text: Establish and operate continuous monitoring programs for deployed AI systems to track operational performance, detect degradation and behavioral drift.

dcwf.top_element_label: Skill in conducting audits or reviews of technical systems.

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

top_anchors.element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.
Skill in conducting audits or reviews of technical systems.

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: Included: task maps directly to AI Safety Researcher scope — RSK-specific signals detected ("performance.{0,15} ?:degrad|drift", "continuous monitor", "detect.{0,15} ?:drift|degradat"). STRM composite is moderate (0.4324); note that framework scores routinely underweight risk management and governance vocabulary, so text-based signal carries primary weight here. Seniority is appropriate: the KSA describes practice-level execution, not enterprise strategy.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: External check: no matches in RSK job postings or cert curricula (CRISC, FRM, NIST AI RMF, CISM). Would a working AI Safety Researcher self-report this as required?
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: Challenge answered: the competency is implicit in AI Safety Researcher work (domain affinity 0.82, 3 RSK signal(s)). Framework vocabulary matching systematically underrepresents risk management and governance KSAs.
